metadata.title: States PCS: Odisha GK & General Studies for OPSC and other Odisha Exams

questions.question: Panchayati Raj system was started in Odisha in which year?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj institution (PRI) was established in Odisha covering all villages in 1961.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj was constitutionalized through which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The Panchayati Raj was constitutionalized through the 73rd Amendment Act of 2002.

questions.question: Each unit of a district of Odisha is called which of the following?
questions.explanation: Each district of Odisha is separated into Sub Divisions. It is governed by Sub Divisional Magistrate (SDM) and a Sub Collector.

questions.question: Which of the following districts of Odisha is the largest by area?
questions.explanation: Mayurbhanj is the largest district of Odisha by area and Ganjam is largest by population

questions.question: Which city of Odisha is known as ‘Silver City’?
questions.explanation: Due to the finest filigree, works Cuttack is known as Silver City of Odisha.

questions.question: Paradeep Port is situated in which district of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Paradeep Port is situated in the Jagatsinghpur district. It is located in the confluence of Mahanadi river and the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Changu dance is a famous folk dance form of which of the following tribes of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Changu dance is a famous folk dance form of Juanga tribe. Juanga tribes are found in the Kendujhar districts of Odisha.

questions.question: The architecture of which of the following temple is known as the “Gem of Odisha Architecture”?
questions.explanation: The architecture of Mukteswar Temple is known as the “Gem of Odisha Architecture” or “Kalinga Architecture” for its sculpture and elegant looks.

questions.question: Which among the following is/ are the objectives of Odisha Tourism Policy 2016?1. To promote sustainable tourism and eco-tourism2. To promote Odisha’s incomparable cultural heritage, natural resources and environment3. To promote Odisha as the one-stop destination to experience the cultural heritageChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: Objectives of Odisha Tourism Policy 2016- To promote sustainable tourism and eco-tourism fir inclusive and economic growth, To promote Odisha’s incomparable cultural heritage, natural resources and environment, To promote Odisha as the one-stop destination to experience the cultural heritage so as to boost foreign & domestic tourist.

questions.question: Which among the following languages is/ are closely associated with Odia1. Assamese2. Bengali3. MaithiliChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: Assamese, Bengali, Maithili are closely associated with Odia language. There are different variations of spoken Odia language.
